coverage.contract: Path xattr calls stay inside the materialized rootfs or a declared bind path, and libcow copy-up preserves optional xattrs before metadata mutation.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks the direct rewrite switch and libcow xattr interceptors.

coverage.contract: Create, remove, and rename path arguments are rewritten from guest absolute paths to rootfs or bind host paths before the kernel sees them.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks path-rewrite coverage entries.

coverage.contract: Symlink/link paths are mediated; linkat can copy regular files across Android boundaries, apt runtime symlink creation is emulated, and /proc/self/exe readlink reports the guest exec path.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks linkat/symlinkat emulation and /proc/self/exe handling.

coverage.contract: Open/stat/access/cwd syscalls resolve against guest rootfs state, report guest cwd, preserve ERANGE for short getcwd buffers, and protect the internal rootfs fd from tracee close.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks getcwd emulation, ERANGE path, faccess emulation, rootfd close emulation, and rewrite table coverage.

coverage.contract: Exec paths resolve under rootfs or PATH, scripts route through their interpreter, argv is rebuilt for the bundled loader, and state tracks the guest executable path.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks loader argv rebuild markers and rootfs exec path tracking.

coverage.contract: AF_UNIX connect sockaddr paths that name guest absolute paths are rewritten to rootfs or bind host paths, with sockaddr length updated. AF_UNIX bind rewrite is currently a planned network hook rather than an active direct-exec hook.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks rewrite_unix_sockaddr_arg and records the bind gap explicitly.

coverage.contract: Container-root credential calls are answered from tracee state without changing Android app credentials, and pid reconciliation rejects reused host pids before restart.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks credential emulation hooks and existing runtime-contract pid identity tests.

coverage.contract: Android-blocked startup/probe syscalls return deterministic Linux-compatible fallback errno values so glibc, libuv, and runtime probes continue through portable paths.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks seccomp errno hooks and userland completion suppression.

coverage.contract: NUMA policy calls blocked by Android app seccomp return ENOSYS, matching kernels without NUMA support and allowing OpenBLAS/llama.cpp style callers to fall back.
coverage.fast_check: Static verifier checks the NUMA range and explicit home-node fallback.

non_syscall_contracts.contract: PTY sessions use forkpty plus execve, expose resize/read/write, and return either WEXITSTATUS or negative WTERMSIG; the direct tracer returns 128+signal for signaled root tracees.
Container start reconciles a verified live pid and rejects stale pid reuse using recorded process start time.
libcow covers open/openat/creat/truncate/fopen, chmod/chown/utime/xattr, fd metadata, close, and dup lifecycle copy-up behavior.

heavy_cases.checks: Guest paths in logs remain under rootfs/binds; getcwd returns guest cwd and ERANGE for too-small buffers; open/stat/access errno values match Linux.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; p=/tmp/pdocker-direct-meta; rm -f \\$p; : > \\$p; chmod 600 \\$p; touch -m \\$p; chown 0:0 \\$p 2>/dev/null || true; python3 - <<PY
import os
p=\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-meta-py\"
open(p,\"w\").close()
os.chmod(p,0o640)
os.utime(p,(123456789,123456790))
try: os.setxattr(p,b\"user.pdocker\",b\"ok\")
except (AttributeError,OSError) as e: print(\"xattr_skip\", getattr(e,\"errno\",None))
else: assert os.getxattr(p,b\"user.pdocker\") == b\"ok\"
assert oct(os.stat(p).st_mode & 0o777) == \"0o640\"
print(\"metadata_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: No Android host paths leak into trace logs; metadata changes are visible only through the guest path.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; d=/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut; rm -rf \\$d; mkdir -p \\$d/a; : > \\$d/a/file; mv \\$d/a/file \\$d/renamed; test -f \\$d/renamed; rm \\$d/renamed; rmdir \\$d/a; python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno, os
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
AT_FDCWD=-100; SYS_openat2=437; SYS_name_to_handle_at=264
class OpenHow(ctypes.Structure):
    _fields_=[(\"flags\",ctypes.c_ulonglong),(\"mode\",ctypes.c_ulonglong),(\"resolve\",ctypes.c_ulonglong)]
how=OpenHow(0,0,0)
rc=libc.syscall(SYS_openat2, AT_FDCWD, b\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut\", ctypes.byref(how), ctypes.sizeof(how))
if rc >= 0: os.close(rc)
else: assert ctypes.get_errno() in (errno.ENOSYS, errno.EINVAL, errno.EOPNOTSUPP, errno.EACCES, errno.ENOENT)
rc=libc.syscall(SYS_name_to_handle_at, AT_FDCWD, b\"/tmp/pdocker-direct-mut\", 0, 0, 0)
assert rc == -1
assert ctypes.get_errno() in (errno.EFAULT, errno.ENOSYS, errno.EOPNOTSUPP, errno.EPERM, errno.ENOENT)
print(\"mutation_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Mutations land under the guest rootfs or declared bind target; unsupported probes return documented errno.

heavy_cases.command: PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc \"set -eu; exe=\\\$(readlink /proc/self/exe); test \\\"\\\$exe\\\" = /bin/sh; d=/tmp/pdocker-direct-links; rm -rf \\\$d; mkdir -p \\\$d; echo ok > \\\$d/src; ln \\\$d/src \\\$d/hard; ln -s src \\\$d/sym; test \\\"\\\$(cat \\\$d/hard)\\\" = ok; test \\\"\\\$(readlink \\\$d/sym)\\\" = src; echo proc_links_ok\"'"
heavy_cases.checks: /proc/self/exe reports the guest executable; link/symlink emulations preserve guest-visible behavior.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_EXEC=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / --env PATH=/tmp:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin -- /bin/sh -lc "set -eu; cat > /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe <<SH
#!/bin/sh
echo script_ok
SH
chmod +x /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe; /bin/echo absolute_ok | grep absolute_ok; pdocker-argv-probe alpha beta | grep script_ok; /tmp/pdocker-argv-probe gamma delta | grep script_ok"'"
heavy_cases.checks: argv[0], interpreter arguments, and process output match guest expectations; no rootfs binary is execve'd directly from app data.

heavy_cases.command: Create a host-side AF_UNIX socket bind exposed as a guest bind path, then connect from a direct-runtime process to the guest socket path.
heavy_cases.checks: connect(AF_UNIX) succeeds through the rewritten host path; active bind rewrite remains documented as planned unless a bind hook is added.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import os, sys
assert os.getuid() == 0 and os.geteuid() == 0
os.setuid(123)
assert os.getuid() == 123 and os.geteuid() == 123
if hasattr(os, \"getresuid\"): assert os.getresuid() == (123,123,123)
pid=os.fork()
if pid == 0:
    assert os.getuid() == 123
    os._exit(0)
_, status = os.waitpid(pid, 0)
assert os.WIFEXITED(status) and os.WEXITSTATUS(status) == 0
print(\"pid_credentials_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Credential calls report container-root state and forked tracees inherit credential state; reused pid rejection remains covered by the fast runtime-contract verifier.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
def call(n,*args):
    ctypes.set_errno(0); rc=libc.syscall(n,*args); return rc, ctypes.get_errno()
for nr in (425,426,427,435,436):
    rc,err=call(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc == -1 and err == errno.ENOSYS, (nr,rc,err)
for nr in (99,293):
    rc,err=call(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc in (0,-1), (nr,rc,err)
print(\"startup_fallbacks_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Fallback syscalls return ENOSYS or the documented success emulation without SIGSYS leakage.

heavy_cases.command:
PDOCKER_PACKAGE="${PDOCKER_PACKAGE:-io.github.ryo100794.pdocker}"; ROOTFS="${ROOTFS:?set ROOTFS to the app-visible rootfs path}"; adb shell "run-as $PDOCKER_PACKAGE sh -lc 'cd files && PDOCKER_DIRECT_TRACE_SYSCALLS=1 pdocker-runtime/docker-bin/pdocker-direct run --mode exec --rootfs $ROOTFS --workdir / -- /bin/sh -lc "python3 - <<PY
import ctypes, errno
libc=ctypes.CDLL(None, use_errno=True)
for nr in (235,236,237,238,239,450):
    ctypes.set_errno(0)
    rc=libc.syscall(nr,0,0,0,0,0,0)
    assert rc == -1 and ctypes.get_errno() == errno.ENOSYS, (nr,rc,ctypes.get_errno())
print(\"numa_enosys_ok\")
PY"'"
heavy_cases.checks: Every NUMA policy syscall returns ENOSYS under direct runtime.

heavy_cases.command: Run docker exec -it or the APK terminal PTY path, resize, exit 7, then terminate by signal.
heavy_cases.checks: PTY read/write/resize works; normal exits return raw code; signaled processes report the documented signal-coded result.
Hardlink copy-up protects lower files across writes, metadata, fd operations, and optional xattrs.

known_gaps.reason: pdocker_direct_exec.c currently hooks connect(AF_UNIX) path rewriting. Network docs still mark bind syscall rewrite as planned for port publishing.
